mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the unemployment rate above 5% signals a weakening labor market that reduces consumer spending and corporate profitability, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with UNRATE > 5% are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05), or the mechanism's direction flips during a recession.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in industrial production above 108 signals a strengthening demand for goods and services that boosts corporate profitability and equity prices, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods with INDPRO > 108 are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05), or the mechanism's direction flips during a recession.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in housing starts below 2000 signals a weakening supply of new homes that reduces housing market activity and corporate profitability, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with HOUST < 2000 are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05), or the mechanism's direction flips during a recession.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the federal funds rate above 5% signals a tightening monetary cycle that compresses equity prices and reduces corporate profitability, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with FEDFUNDS > 5% are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05), or the mechanism's direction flips during a recession.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decrease in initial jobless claims below 200,000 signals a strengthening labor market that boosts consumer spending and corporate profitability, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with PAYEMS < 200,000 are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05), or the mechanism's direction flips during a recession.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in real GDP above 11000 signals a strengthening demand for goods and services that boosts corporate profitability and equity prices, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with GDPC1 > 11000 are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05), or the mechanism's direction flips during a recession.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in real GDP above 11000 and a 3-month increase in industrial production above 108 signals a strengthening supply of goods and services that boosts corporate profitability and equity prices, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with GDPC1 > 11000 and INDPRO > 108 are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05), or the mechanism's direction flips during a recession.
